```{r}
#install.packages("networkD3")
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(rlang) 
library(tidyr)
library(networkD3)

```
```{r}
sankeydata2<- readr::read_csv('../data/processed/quotations.csv')
sankeydata2<- sankeydata2%>%select("Document","Codes")
#sankeydata2<-sankeydata2%>%group_by(Document,Codes)%>%summarise(Value=n())
codes_to_exclude <- c("Paper Reflection: Comment", "Transitions: Academic Community","Transitions: Legitimize Design (Scientific)","NA","Transitions: Digital Tech Connections")
sankeydata2<-sankeydata2%>%filter(!Codes %in% codes_to_exclude)%>%
  # 1. Separate and expand the rows based on the 'Code' column
  separate_rows(Codes, sep = ",") 
#%>% filter(Document%in%"Culture-Orientated Food Design for Social Issue.pdf")

```

```{r}

# Assuming your original data frame is 'df' with columns 'Document' and 'Code'

# 1. Create the Contribution data frame
contributions <- sankeydata2 %>%
  filter(startsWith(Codes, "Contribution:")) %>%
  rename(Contribution = Codes) %>%
  select(Document, Contribution) # Keep only Document and the new Contribution column

# 2. Create the Research Method data frame
methods <- sankeydata2  %>%
  filter(startsWith(Codes, "Research Method:")) %>%
  rename(Method = Codes) %>%
  select(Document, Method) # Keep only Document and the new Method column

# Join the two data frames on the 'Document' column
# This creates a row for every unique (Contribution, Method) pair within a document
paired_data <- contributions %>%
  inner_join(methods, by = "Document") %>%
  # Remove the 'Document' column temporarily, as we only need the pairs for counting
  select(Contribution, Method)

# Group the pairs and count the frequency of each unique combination
combination_counts <- paired_data %>%
  group_by(Contribution, Method) %>%
  summarise(
    Count = n(), # Count the number of rows (i.e., documents) for each combination
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Count), Contribution, Method) # Optional: Sort by count

colnames(combination_counts)<-c("Source","Target","Value")
#combination_counts$Source<-as.factor(combination_counts$Source)
#unique(combination_counts$Source)
```
```{r}
# 1. Create a distinct list of all unique nodes (titles and codes)
nodes <- data.frame(
  name = unique(c(combination_counts$Source, combination_counts$Target))
)%>%
  mutate(
    Group = case_when(
      startsWith(name, "Contribution: Theoretical") ~ "Theoretical",
      startsWith(name, "Contribution: Methodological") ~ "Methodological",
      startsWith(name, "Contribution: Practical/Applied") ~ "Practical/Applied",
      startsWith(name, "Contribution: Summative/Integrative") ~ "Summative/Integrative",
      startsWith(name, "Contribution: Empirical") ~ "Empirical",
      startsWith(name, "Research Method: Qualitative") ~ "Qualitative",
      startsWith(name, "Research Method: Quantitative") ~ "Quantitative",
      TRUE ~ "Other" # A fallback group for any unexpected nodes
    ))
  

```

```{r}
# Order nodes: Contributions first, then Methods, alphabetically within each group
nodes <- nodes %>%
  mutate(
    GroupOrder = case_when(
      startsWith(Group, "Theoretical") | startsWith(Group, "Methodological") | 
      startsWith(Group, "Practical") | startsWith(Group, "Summative") | 
      startsWith(Group, "Empirical") ~ 1,  # Contributions come first
      TRUE ~ 2  # Methods come second
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(GroupOrder, Group, name) %>%
  select(-GroupOrder)  # Remove the temporary ordering column

```

```{r}

# 2. Create the numerical ID column (0-indexed)
nodes$node_id <- 0:(nrow(nodes) - 1)

# 3. Map the numerical IDs back to the combination_counts edge list
links <- combination_counts %>%
  # Match Source names to their ID
  left_join(nodes, by = c("Source" = "name")) %>%
  rename(source_id = node_id) %>%
  # Match Target names to their ID
  left_join(nodes, by = c("Target" = "name")) %>%
  rename(target_id = node_id) %>%
  # Keep only the required columns for networkD3
  select(source = source_id, target = target_id, value = Value)


```

```{r}
# Create the Sankey Diagram
sankey_diagram <- sankeyNetwork(
  # Link data frame
  Links = links,
  # Node data frame
  Nodes = nodes,
  # Columns specifying the source and target node IDs
  Source = "source",
  Target = "target",
  # Column specifying the link thickness/weight
  Value = "value",
  # Column containing the node labels/names
  NodeID = "name",
  NodeGroup = "Group",
  # Control the diagram width and height (optional)
  width = 900,
  height = 700,
  fontSize = 14,
  fontFamily = "Arial"

)

# Display the interactive diagram (it will open in a browser or RStudio Viewer)
sankey_diagram

```

```{r}

# Install if needed: install.packages("htmlwidgets")
library(htmlwidgets)

# Assuming your Sankey diagram object is named 'sankey_diagram'
# Save the diagram as an interactive HTML file
saveWidget(
  sankey_diagram,
  file = "../site/html/coding-analysis_sankey.html",
  libdir = NULL,
  selfcontained = TRUE # IMPORTANT: embeds all JavaScript/CSS data into the file
)
```
